LAW AND MOTION 302, DEDENDANTS AMERICAN APPAREL, INC, SEAN ALONZO'S PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD. ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI; REPORTER: ANGIE DINER, CSR 	581 - AFTER HEARING THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT'S PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD IS DENIED. IT IS CLEAR FROM THE RECORD AND TRANSCRIPT THAT THE ARBITRATOR CONSIDERED THE EVIDENCE AND ARGUMENT PRESENTED BY DEFENDANT REGARDING THE RELEASES AND REJECTED THESE RELEASES AS A DEFENSE. FURTHER, DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION TO THE DECLARATION OF BARBARA FIGARI IS OVERRULED. PARTIES NOTIFIED OF THE COURTS RULING BY TELEPHONE. PLAINTIFF IS DIRECTED TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER.

GIORGI; REPORTER: ANGIE DINER, CSR 	581 - AFTER HEARING THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: PETITION TO CONFIRM THE ARBITRATION AWARD IS GRANTED. PARTIES NOTIFIED OF THE COURT'S RULING BY TELEPHONE. PLAINTIFF IS DIRECTED TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER.

Notice On Petition And Petition O Vacate Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Aug-22-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEDENDANTS AMERICAN APPAREL, INC, SEAN ALONZO'S PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD.
Appearances: Larry Organ & Barbara Figari of Equality Lawyers (453.4740) for plaintiff and responding party; Joyce E. Crucillo of American Apparel (213.272.9642) for defendants and moving parties.
Argued and the court took the matter under submission. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Reporter: Angie Diner, CSR 581
---
After hearing the court ruled as follows: Defendant's petition to vacate arbitration award is denied. It is clear from the record and transcript that the arbitrator considered the evidence and argument presented by defendant regarding the releases and rejected these releases as a defense. Further, defendant's objection to the declaration of Barbara Figari is overruled. Parties notified of the courts ruling by telephone. Plaintiff is directed to submit a proposed form of order.

Notice Of Petition And Petition To Confirm Arb Awarda Nd Enter Judgmet

MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHER RENFRO'S PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD AND ENTER JUDGMENT.
Appearances: Larry Organ & Barbara Figari of Equality Lawyers (453.4740) for plaintiff and moving party; Joyce E. Crucillo of American Apparel (213.272.9642) for defendants and responding parties.
Argued and the court took the matter under submission. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Reporter: Angie Diner, CSR 581
---
After hearing the court ruled as follows: petition to confirm the arbitration award is granted. Parties notified of the court's ruling by telephone. Plaintiff is directed to submit a proposed form of order.

Off Calendar Oct-24-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Barbara Figari of Figari Law (235.2100) for plaintiff and responding party
Joyce Crucillo of American Apparel, Inc. (213.271.9560) appearing via Court Call for defendants and moving parties

Argued and the Court modified its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS AMERICAN APPAREL, INC AND SEAN ALONZO'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION is granted. Arbitration shall take place in San Francisco per the first arbitration agreement. Per the subsequent arbitration agreements, all fees are to be paid by employer. Ms. Crucillo to prepare a form of order. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600
